title: [skip-ci] RPM: set BUILD_ORIGIN
body:
For Copr builds, it will mention the Copr info from where the rpm is
installed.

For non-copr builds, it will mention the value of the packager macro
if set, and skip this field altogether if not.

On local rpm builds, this shows:
```
Build Origin:  Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>
```

On koji rpm builds, this shows:
```
Build Origin: Fedora Project
```

On copr rpm builds (for eg. rhcontainerbot/playground), this shows:
```
Build Origin: Copr: rhcontainerbot/playground
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

```
$ sudo dnf -y copr enable packit/containers-podman-25235
$ sudo dnf -y update podman
$ podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.5.0-dev-50163aedd
API Version:   5.5.0-dev-50163aedd
Go Version:    go1.24rc2
Built:         Wed Feb  5 05:30:00 2025
Build Origin:  packit/containers-podman-25235
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```
LGTM, assuming no additional requirements are needed to assure `/usr/lib/rpm/macros.d/macros.dist `will always be present on the system during the build.
F42 just branched so there's some dnf repo issues causing failures. Shouldn't block this PR.

> LGTM, assuming no additional requirements are needed to assure `/usr/lib/rpm/macros.d/macros.dist `will always be present on the system during the build.

It's provided by `redhat-release`, `centos-stream-release` and `fedora-release-common`. The first two are protected packages on base containers, so I'm guessing they'll always be there. Removing `fedora-release-common` fails because that will remove protected packages like `setup` and `sudo`, so I'm guessing that'll always be there too.
Not merging so some others can take a look at the name in case they prefer something else.

In the latest diff, I've used a macro as it was a lot simpler in spec.

For non-copr builds, the value of `%{packager}` will be used if set. So, all builds done on koji will show `Build Origin: Fedora Project` regardless of the human / automation triggering the build. Also see updated PR description.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 3c3baefa8c2b989402dc02bd378218d53e45383b. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
